blocks.text: Address: 5501 E Harmon Ave Apt 181 Las Vegas, NV 89122-
*Pertinent documentation to follow, please notify if additional information is required*
RIGHT SHODULDER ARTHROSCOPIC ROTATOR CUFF REPAIR - SEE TITACHED
EDWARD S. ASHMAN, M.D. - BRADLEY S. BAKER, M.D. - MICHAEL S. BRADFORD, M.D. - HOLMAN CHAN, M.D. - KEVIN GOLSHANI, M.D.
RONALD W. HILLOCK, M.D. - SHAIN L. HOWARD, D.O. - KEITH W. KOTECKI, M.D. - KEVIN LAROCHE, M.D. - JUSTIN LIMTONG, D.O.
ZEFU LIU, D.O. - CHINEDU O. NWASIKE, M.D. - REYNOLD L. RIMOLDI, M.D. - MICHAEL ROSSIDIS, D.P.M.
KEVIN R. SHARIF, M.D. - ARTHUR J. TAYLOR, M.D. - MICHAEL D. THOMAS, M.D.
Ashley Harrington, PA-C - Simon Hwang, PA-C - Dalton Laut, PA-C - Paul A. Morgan, PA-C - Lucas Porter, PA-C
Heather A. Rhoden, PA-C - Jessica Russian, APRN - Jason A. Zarn, PA-C - Jay Zurfluh, PA-C
The patient is a 62 year old female presenting to discuss diagnostic procedure results. The patient had an MRI.
meloxicam (7.5mg tablet, 1 (one) oral twice a day, Taken starting 07/09/2025) Active.
Anoro Ellipta (62.5-25mcg/actuat Blister, With I, inhalation) Active.
albuterol sulfate (90mcg/actuat HFA Aerosol Inh, inhalation) Active.
No pertinent past surgical history Phreesia 07/02/2025
Current work status: Not Currently Employed Phreesia 07/02/2025
Assistance Status: Home Assistance Available Phreesia 07/02/2025
Surgical Blood Transfusion: Does not approve surgical blood transfusion Phreesia 07/02/2025
Alcohol use: Occasional Alcohol Use Phreesia 07/02/2025
Contract: No Active Pain Contract Phreesia 07/02/2025
Drug Assessment: No Illicit Drug Use Phreesia 07/02/2025
History Note: She has right shoulder pain since she was knocked over by dogs
She has multiple stents and valves and cannot get an MRI
She recently got injections in her neck with pain management
Right shoulder CT arthrogram shows a full-thickness rotator cuff tear 15 mm X 15 mm
General Not Present- Chills and Fever.
Gastrointestinal Not Present- Diarrhea and Nausea.
Neurological Not Present- Loss of Consciousness.
Chief complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness
General: Awake, alert, oriented, appropriate. Skin intact, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic pupils equal round and reactive ears clear
The right shoulder was examined, there is no redness there is no warmth. Patient is neurologically intact. Passive forward
flexion is full and active active forward flexion is 90°. Passive lateral abduction is full and active lateral abduction is 90°. Mild
tenderness to palpation over the deltoid tuberosity. Nontender to palpation over the a.c. joint. Brisk capillary refill one
through 5 intact. NVI over distal upper extremity. Has a positive drop arm test.
CT arthrogram was reviewed including images and there is a full-thickness rotator cuff tear.
Warned of the risks of surgery including but not limited to death stroke disability blood clot infection and septic arthritis
osteomyelitis re tear need for repeat surgery. Arthrofibrosis. Rotator cuff re teaar. All questions were asked and answered.
I explained that no guarantees can be made regarding the results of surgery.
The patient was warned not to mix pain medication with any other pain medications. Patient has signed an agreement
stating that if they receive pain medication from the hospital not receive pain medication from any other providers. Patient
was warned not to mix pain medication with alcohol, nor to take at the same time as operating heavy machinery, or any
activity in which they need to be of sound mind. They were instructed to take the pain medication only as directed.
Assessment is right rotator cuff tear chronic
Plan is right arthroscopic rotator cuff repair of a chronic retracted tear
I explained to the patient that she does not followup for rotator cuff tear and does not get surgery in a timely fashion one
potential risk is that the rotator cuff tear could become irreparable which would change surgical options
Pre-op studies to be completed by PCP
Electronically signed by: EDWARD S. ASHMAN MD
Patient understanding of restrictions and limitations

Ice often to reduce swelling and pain.
